title: GitLab Down 35% in Six Months: Should You Buy, Sell or Hold the Stock?
text: GitLab (GTLB) shares have declined 34.8% over the past six months, underperforming the broader tech sector's 7.9% gain. The company faces headwinds including challenges from U.S. public sector delays due to government shutdown and ongoing budget constraints. Despite near-term challenges, GitLab shows promise through its expanding enterprise customer base. High-value customers with more than $5K ARR reached 10,475 in Q3 FY2026, a 10% year-over-year increase. The company's AI-powered GitLab Duo platform and strategic partnerships with Amazon Web Services and Google Cloud position it competitively in the DevSecOps market. For FY2026, GitLab projects 25% revenue growth to $946-947 million and non-GAAP earnings of 88-89 cents per share. The stock trades at a premium valuation of 4.4X forward P/S versus industry average of 3.9X. The stock faces intense competition from Microsoft GitHub and Atlassian. GitLab carries a Zacks Rank #4 (Sell) rating, suggesting investors should avoid the stock currently despite its strong technological fundamentals.
